channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Hegseth’s Inner Circle Expands with Wife and Allies as Pentagon Faces Leadership Purge
item.description: Pete Hegseth, the Secretary of Defense, has come under scrutiny after dismissing more than two dozen Pentagon officials in recent weeks. As the leader of the U.S. military’s efforts in the Iran war, H...

item.title: Joint rescue operation launched after two US soldiers go missing during drills in Morocco
item.description: Authorities in Morocco and the United States have initiated a coordinated search-and-rescue operation after two American service members went missing during the African Lion military exercises near Ta...

item.title: Family Recalls How Pastor Assisted in Finding Mercy Wanjiru After Three-Month Disappearance
item.description: In Nairobi, Mercy Wanjiru went missing for three months after leaving for a party in Utawala, and her family struggled to find her. Her sister, Ciku Kimani, shared with Kenya Digital News that despite...

item.title: Tiwa Savage Talks About How Don Jazzy Helped Her Transition From R&B To Afrobeats
item.description: Nigerian singer Tiwa Savage don yarn about how e no easy for her to waka from R&B to Afrobeats after she come back Nigeria in 2012. She start her music career abroad as backup singer for big internati...

item.title: United Airlines 767 Hits Truck and Streetlight During Newark Landing; No Passengers Injured
item.description: A United Airlines Boeing 767 traveling from Venice, Italy, collided with a truck and a highway streetlight while landing at Newark Liberty International Airport on May 3, 2026. The incident occurred n...

item.title: Fr. Nick Vaquilar appointed as new bishop of Urdaneta Diocese
item.description: Pope Leo XIV has officially appointed Fr. Nick Vaquilar of the Archdiocese of Nueva Segovia as the new bishop of the Diocese of Urdaneta, following the resignation of Bishop Jacinto Jose. The announce...

item.title: Rudy Giuliani hospitalized in critical condition, according to Trump
item.description: Rudy Giuliani, former Mayor of New York City and former personal lawyer to Donald Trump, has reportedly been hospitalized and is in critical condition. According to statements made by Donald Trump, Gi...

item.title: Philes Ongori claims second Toronto Marathon victory after seeking asylum in Canada
item.description: Philes Ongori, a Kenyan long-distance runner, secured her second victory at the Toronto Marathon on Sunday, demonstrating remarkable resilience since arriving in Canada as an asylum seeker in 2023. On...

item.title: Ensuring Bargaining Council Compliance for Safer Workforce Partnerships
item.description: In South Africa's evolving labour landscape, compliance with bargaining councils has become a critical business priority rather than a mere administrative duty. Employers now face not only standard ob...

item.title: NCAA DG addresses concerns about flight disruptions and passenger care in Nigeria
item.description: The Director-General of the Nigerian Civil Aviation Authority (NCAA), Chris Najomo, has stated that flight delays and cancellations are a global issue, and will continue in Nigeria’s aviation sector. ...

item.title: Planning Minister Assures Support to Investors Amid Chinese Company Concerns
item.description: In Islamabad, Federal Planning and Development Minister Ahsan Iqbal called a high-level meeting after Han Geng Group, a Chinese company, announced plans to shut down its operations in Pakistan and Chi...

item.title: Diplomatic Deadlock Prolongs Iran Conflict
item.description: The conflict between the US and Iran, now entering its third month, remains in a delicate state of ceasefire, with no clear resolution in sight. Pakistan has been actively mediating between the two si...

item.title: U.S. to Assist Ships Stranded in the Strait of Hormuz Starting Monday
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced that the United States will begin assisting ships stranded in the Strait of Hormuz starting on Monday. The Strait, a critical maritime passage for global oil ship...

item.title: One-Minute Cancer Jab Set to Revolutionise NHS Cancer Treatment
item.description: A new one-minute cancer injection is being introduced by the NHS, offering a faster alternative to the traditional intravenous drip. This immunotherapy treatment, which instructs the body’s immune sys...

item.title: George Ruto Celebrates Award in Viral Matatu Video Amid Safety Concerns
item.description: A video of George Kimutai, President William Ruto’s youngest son, celebrating aboard his flashy Baba Yaga matatu has gone viral. The celebration, which took place on May 2, followed his recognition fo...

item.title: Wu Yize takes 10-7 lead over Shaun Murphy on opening day of World Snooker final
item.description: Chinese snooker prodigy Wu Yize made a commanding start in his first World Snooker Championship final at the Crucible, establishing a 10-7 lead over 2005 champion Shaun Murphy after the opening day. D...

item.title: Early Trends in Kerala Assembly Elections Show UDF Leading in Initial Counting
item.description: The counting of votes for the 2026 Kerala Assembly elections began on Monday at 8 am. Early trends indicate that the Congress-led United Democratic Front (UDF) is leading in ten constituencies, while ...

item.title: Andaman and Nicobar Islands achieve Guinness records with underwater human stack and flag display
item.description: The Andaman and Nicobar Islands have achieved a notable feat by setting two Guinness World Records within a span of two days, showcasing a blend of coordination, endurance, and national pride. The hig...

item.title: Daughter of Bondi Beach terror victim gives evidence at Antisemitism royal commission
item.description: The Royal Commission into Antisemitism in Australia has begun its public hearings, with one of the first witnesses being Sheina Gutnick, daughter of Reuven Morrison, who was killed in the Bondi Beach ...
